results.title: Biggest Puzzle in Computer Science: P vs. NP
results.reason: High production value and a narrative-driven transcript make this the most accessible entry point for a beginner to understand the 'why' behind the problem.
summary: Learn the difference between easy and hard problems
Understand why checking answers is faster than solving
Explore the most famous unsolved math mystery
Discover how these puzzles affect modern computer security

results.title: 8. NP-Hard and NP-Complete Problems
results.reason: Abdul Bari is renowned for pedagogical excellence; the transcript shows a dedicated focus on clearing confusion for students through structured logic.
summary: Identify problems that take too long to solve
Learn how to group difficult tasks by complexity
Understand the meaning of hard and complete categories
See how different math problems relate to each other

results.title: NP Hard and NP Complete Problems, Non Deterministic Algorithms |DAA|
results.reason: The transcript indicates a very structured approach covering non-deterministic algorithms and reduction, which are essential for a beginner's foundation.
summary: Learn how computers make guesses to find answers
Understand the basics of hard to solve problems
Discover how to verify if a solution is correct
Explore simple ways to categorize different computer tasks

results.title: What is a polynomial-time reduction? (NP-Hard + NP-complete)
results.reason: Focuses specifically on the 'reduction' aspect which is the hardest part for beginners to grasp, providing necessary technical depth.
summary: Learn how to turn one problem into another
Understand why some problems are equally difficult
See how shortcuts help classify complex computer tasks
Discover the link between simple and hard puzzles

results.title: Proving P=NP Requires Concepts We Don't Have | Richard Karp and Lex Fridman
results.reason: Features a Turing Award winner; great for high-level conceptual inspiration but less effective for learning the specific mechanics of NP-Hardness.
summary: Hear why this famous puzzle remains unsolved today
Learn why current math tools are not enough
Understand the limits of our current computer knowledge
Explore the future of solving very difficult problems
